# Meta data

<pre>
Ramon benchmark for Z3
-
Job description: QF_LIA full, largest cube test disabled (baseline)
Job tag: lcube_off_qflia_200_rs
Runner: rise-runner-2
Z3 repo: Z3Prover/z3
Z3 commit: 507a67984066e13c1574bd145d318a62dfe6613b
Z3 branch: lcube
Z3 options: "-T:200 smt.random_seed=601 lp.lcube=false"
Z3 inputs: https://zenodo.org/records/16740866/files/QF_LIA.tar.zst?download=1
Z3 commit message: Throttle the largest cube test exponentially on failure

The QF_LIA runs comparing lp.lcube=true against the baseline (runs
2326-2329, seeds 11 and 58) show the test pays off on cube-friendly
families (slacks -354s/-284s, 20180326-Bromberger -353s/-561s, dillig,
CAV_2009) but burns time retrying forever on families where no large
cube exists (convert +878s/+879s, cut_lemmas +245s/+217s, rings
+209s/+207s), costing 22/28 solved instances.

If the polyhedron does not contain a large enough cube, it will not
contain one later either, after more constraints are added. So gate
find_largest_cube() by its own period m_lcube_period, initialized to
m_int_find_cube_period: double it (capped at 2^30) each time the test
returns undef and reset it on success, following the hnf_cut_period
pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

</pre>
